questions.stem: <ruby>毎日<rt>まいにち</rt></ruby><ruby>練習<rt>れんしゅう</rt></ruby>して、<ruby>泳<rt>およ</rt></ruby>げる＿＿＿。
questions.why: Potential verb + ようになる marks a change that HAPPENED to you — you became able to swim. ようにした would mean you deliberately made a point of swimming; ことにした is a decision you made.

questions.stem: <ruby>子<rt>こ</rt></ruby>どもが<ruby>一人<rt>ひとり</rt></ruby>で<ruby>服<rt>ふく</rt></ruby>を<ruby>着<rt>き</rt></ruby>られる＿＿＿。
questions.why: A gradual change of ability, not a decision or an arrangement. ことになりました would mean it was decided (by someone) that the child dresses himself.

questions.stem: <ruby>勉強<rt>べんきょう</rt></ruby>を<ruby>続<rt>つづ</rt></ruby>けて、<ruby>日本語<rt>にほんご</rt></ruby>のニュースが<ruby>分<rt>わ</rt></ruby>かる＿＿＿。
questions.why: The change-of-state reading: understanding came about over time. わけだ draws a conclusion; ところだ is 'about to / just now'.
